data.body:
PASS-1 review — T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings.

T3 fires only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a never-before-used capability. This plan (reinstate per-pass latency/cost telemetry on the WORKFLOW plan-review gate) integrates EXCLUSIVELY with rebar's own internal, already-used workflow machinery — no external surface and no asserted-but-unused capability — so per the overlay's gating clause and ANTI-FP rule ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable") it does not engage.

Every capability the plan's ACs name was verified present and already exercised in-tree (none is asserted-but-unverified):
- AC1 names the metrics being "captured from the workflow run (recorder step timings / the batch_plan coverage)". The recorder's per-step timing is an EXISTING interpreter capability: the interpreter records `duration_ms` per step (src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py:388, 489; `_maybe_record_control` ... `duration_ms=duration_ms`) and the executor persists it (src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py:655, 672-673). MATCH (internal).
- The `batch_plan` finder's `criteria_count` output the cost-proxy reads is real and pre-existing: emitted by the batch runner (src/rebar/llm/workflow/runners.py:136; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py:134) and consumed at gate_dispatch.py:157. MATCH (internal).
- Step `kind` partitioning (`precheck` DET tier vs `agent`/`batch` LLM tier) the reconstruction relies on is an existing recorder field. MATCH (internal).
- The retired bespoke producer (B-RETIRE / `orchestrator.run_review`, db7b AC5 coverage['metrics'] = {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, claim_path}) is real prior art the plan reconstructs equivalently — not a new external dependency.

Auth/HTTPS preconditions — not-applicable: no external/network surface is introduced (the captured cost proxy is per-pass review work, and the claim-path marker is explicitly "no-llm/no-network (local HMAC verify)").
SPIKE-on-critical-gap — not-applicable: no critical capability gap exists; the integration is achievable on existing primitives, so no SPIKE is warranted.

Affirmations (T3 checklist):
- The integration is achievable as described — every named mechanism (recorder step timings/`duration_ms`, `batch_plan`/`criteria_count`, step-kind tiering) is present in-tree and already used. No capability gap.
- Named subcommands/endpoints actually exist — MATCH (internal): the recorder timing fields and batch coverage outputs are implemented and exercised by existing tests/runners.
- Auth/HTTPS preconditions — not-applicable (no external endpoint).
- Critical-gap → SPIKE — not-applicable (no gap).

No T3 findings.
